name: Interval Scheduling and Merge
description: Implement an IntervalManager class that supports adding intervals, merging overlapping ones, querying if a point is covered, and finding the minimum number of points to cover all intervals (hitting set). When intervals are added, they should automatically merge with any overlapping existing intervals.
tests:
def test_add_and_merge():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 3)
    m.add_interval(2, 5)
    assert m.get_merged() == [(1, 5)]

def test_non_overlapping():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 2)
    m.add_interval(4, 6)
    assert m.get_merged() == [(1, 2), (4, 6)]

def test_multiple_merges():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 3)
    m.add_interval(5, 7)
    m.add_interval(2, 6)
    assert m.get_merged() == [(1, 7)]

def test_is_covered():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 5)
    m.add_interval(8, 10)
    assert m.is_covered(3) == True
    assert m.is_covered(6) == False
    assert m.is_covered(9) == True

def test_min_hitting_set():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 4)
    m.add_interval(3, 6)
    m.add_interval(8, 10)
    points = m.min_hitting_set()
    assert len(points) == 2
    for s, e in m.get_merged():
        assert any(s <= p <= e for p in points)

def test_adjacent_intervals():
    m = IntervalManager()
    m.add_interval(1, 3)
    m.add_interval(3, 5)
    assert m.get_merged() == [(1, 5)]

def test_empty():
    m = IntervalManager()
    assert m.get_merged() == []
    assert m.min_hitting_set() == []
constraints: get_merged returns sorted list of non-overlapping intervals
min_hitting_set returns smallest set of points covering all intervals
